title: Inventory Management: Change Inventory Attributes via Import

export_view.value:
The Inventory Detail screen allows users to manage and update inventory attributes. Each inventory item has individual records that can be updated. Users with the Inventory Manager role or higher can make bulk adjustments to their inventory using an Import Template. This enables bulk changes to attributes such as Inventory Status, Condition, Current Location, Supplier, Price, Cost, and Carrier. Inventory status updates can be made for items in Live and Transitional status. Items already in Consumed status cannot be changed to maintain data integrity.

Import Rules

Inventory Status - Inventory status refers to the current state of an inventory item, indicating whether it is available for use (Live), in the process of being updated or moved (Transition), or no longer sellable (Consumed).

  • You can only update the attributes of items currently in a Live or Transition status.

  • Items in a Consumed status (Sold, Voided, Shrinkage, etc.) cannot be changed.

  • See our article, Inventory Status - What Does it Mean? for more information.

Column Matching - Columns are matched by header name. We recommend using the header names provided in our templates to ensure a successful import of the column values. Any columns added outside the provided template will be ignored.

Accepted File Formats - RepairQ accepts Excel (.xlsx), Comma-Separated Values (.csv), and Tab-Separated Values (.txt). We recommend using .xlsx files for the best upload experience.

Additional Resources

  • Inventory Management Overview

  • Inventory Management: Manual Inventory Attribute Changes

  • RepairQ Data Import Resources

Downloading the Inventory Detail Template

On the RepairQ Navigation Bar, go to Inventory → Inventory Detail.

On the Inventory Detail page, the list will show all items added to inventory within the last 90 days, however, you can filter results for the batch of inventory items you would like to adjust.

Note: We recommend paring down the inventory list before downloading the import template, as the template will include all results from the current filtered results.

Once your new results have populated, click the Options button near the top-right of the page. From the list of options, click Import.

A new section will appear below the summary list — Import Inventory Changes. Click Inventory Detail Import Template to download an Excel spreadsheet of your filtered results. Save this file somewhere on your computer that is easily accessible to make changes and re-upload.

Updating the Import Template

For a successful import, you must use the exact header names provided in the import template. Matches are made based on the Inventory ID. Any changes to field data must match existing values within the application, except for Cost, Price, Serial Number, and Notes. For instance, if you update the Supplier and Carrier fields, the values must correspond to existing categories. New categories cannot be created through this import process.

Import Fields

  • ID - The Inventory ID for the line item. Do not change this field, since this is used to identify each item.

  • Location - The assigned location for the inventory item. If updating this field, use the location Short Name assigned in Location Settings.

  • Catalog Item - The assigned Catalog Item name. If updating this field, you must use an existing Catalog Item of the same Item Type.

  • Serial Number - The assigned serial number, if applicable. You cannot add a serial number to a non-serialized item.

  • Status - The inventory item’s Inventory Status. You can update any Live or Transition status to any other status, however, Items in a Consumed status (Sold, Voided, Shrinkage, etc.) cannot be changed.

  • Supplier - The supplying location/source of these items. If updating this field, you must use an existing supplier.

  • Carrier - This is the carrier network provider for the item. If updating this field, you must use an existing carrier.

  • Cost - This is the cost of the item from the Supplier.

  • Price - This is the retail price of the item.

  • Condition - This is the current condition of the item. If updating this field, you must use an existing condition.

  • Note - A comment or remark to the item's record for future reference or clarification. This can include information like special handling instructions, reasons for status changes, or any other relevant details.

Importing the Template and Import Feedback Page

When you have saved the file and are ready to upload your import, click Choose File and select your file from the file explorer. Once the file is selected, click Import. RepairQ will begin processing your import file.

Note: Larger files may take some time to process. Please be patient and do not navigate away from the page.

Import Feedback

When finished, you will be redirected to the import feedback page. This page will inform you of the success or failure of each row in your import file. If a line in the document fails to upload, you will receive a message outlining why it failed.

START 1.21.X
